Authoritative results already exist on other machines: FAIL on Daniel-PC (2026-04-05T23:48Z) and FAIL on EWIN-PC (2026-04-06T02:28Z). non_actionable_errors_audited.title: INV-053: Depression Attractor Replication (SD-018)
non_actionable_errors_audited.non_actionable_reason: Duplicate-machine run killed by SIGTERM. Authoritative result already exists: PASS on EWIN-PC (2026-04-07T02:29Z, ~10h before the kill). Same SIGTERM-on-cloud pattern observed in V3-EXQ-490 (handled by 2026-04-27T05:25 diagnose-errors session) -- shared-vCPU OOM neighbour pressure or transient cloud scheduler kill. Not a code crash. Evidence dir v3_exq_249_inv053_depression_attractor_replication/ already carries the EWIN-PC PASS.
non_actionable_errors_audited.recommended_disposition: Governance: add to review_tracker.discussed_experiment_dirs in next /governance walk. The PASS run is already authoritative.

summary: 0 ERRORs require a fix script. 3 ERRORs flagged for governance: duplicate-machine run artifacts on ree-cloud-1 where the experiment already has authoritative outcomes on other machines (FAIL/FAIL/PASS). 28 other ERRORs already in discussed_experiment_dirs.
next_actions: Governance: add V3-EXQ-125a, V3-EXQ-247, V3-EXQ-249b to review_tracker.discussed_experiment_dirs in next /governance walk.

Optional infrastructure follow-up: the multi-machine claim race let ree-cloud-1 pick up duplicate work after another machine had completed (same root cause pattern as the V3-EXQ-490 SIGTERM noted on 2026-04-27T05:25Z). The dedup-on-claim path may have a small window where two machines pull work before the first push lands.
